LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT VISA INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT CERTAIN UNDERWRITERS AT LLOYD'S LONDON'S 1) DUTY TO DEFEND VISA INC., 2) FOURTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE AND 3) CROSS-CLAIM COUNTS I-VI. THE COURT MADE A PERSONAL DISCLOSURE AND NO OBJECTIONS WERE RAISED. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED AS TO ISSUE ONE AND GRANTED AS TO THE REMAINING ISSUES. PER THE PARTIES' INSURANCE CONTRACT, DEFENDANT DID NOT HAVE AN IMMEDIATE DUTY TO PAY FOR PLAINTIFF'S DEFENSE COSTS BECAUSE THE $10 MILLION CLASS ACTION DEDUCTIBLE COVERS BOTH DAMAGES AND DEFENSE COSTS. FURTHER, PER THE PARTIES' INSURANCE CONTRACT, THE MONETARY AMOUNTS AWARDED PURSUANT TO PENAL CODE 637.2(A) AND THE APPLICABLE PROVISIONS OF THE LAWS OF THE OTHER STATES ARE NEITHER "PENALTIES" NOR "SANCTIONS." ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

PLAINTIFF VISA INC.'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Against Defendants; Request For Sanctions - The court adopts in full the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa as follows: Denied as to Requests 3, 4 and 6 and granted as to Requests 5, 14, 17 and 18 at the option of defendants to produce them or for defendant to make available a custodian of record. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jan-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT VISA INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT CERTAIN UNDERWRITERS AT LLOYD'S LONDON'S 1) DUTY TO DEFEND VISA INC., 2) FOURTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE AND 3) CROSS-CLAIM COUNTS I-VI.
Appearances: David A. Thomas and Robin L. Cohen of Kasowitz Benson Torres & Friedman LLP (421.6140) for plaintiff and moving party; Barry A. Chasnoff of Akin Gump Strauss Hauer & Feld LLP (210.281.7001) and Peter J. Whalen of Clyde & Co. 365.9860) for defendant and responding party.
The court made a personal disclosure and no objections were raised. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied as to issue one and granted as to the remaining issues. Per the parties' insurance contract, defendant did not have an immediate duty to pay for plaintiff's defense costs because the $10 million class action deductible covers both damages and defense costs. Further, per the parties' insurance contract, the monetary amounts awarded pursuant to Penal Code 637.2(a) and the applicable provisions of the laws of the other states are neither "penalties" nor "sanctions." Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings; Req. For Judicial Ntc
Off Calendar Jan-06-2012 Text Ruling

The court made a personal disclosure and no objections were raised. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Per the parties' insurance contract, monetary amounts awarded pursuant to Penal Code 637.2(a) are neither "penalties" nor "sanctions." Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077
